SEARCH
Package configs

Class MathExampleConfig

java.lang.Object
configs.MathExampleConfig
All Implemented Interfaces:
Config
public class MathExampleConfig extends Object implements Config
A hardcoded computational math layout configuration that sets up a multi-stage calculation pipeline.

This class implements the Config interface to deploy a static math-processing graph. It instantiates and chains three BinOpAgent instances using intermediate subscription topics to execute the algebraic expression:

R3 = (A + B) * (A - B)

Data Flow Routing:

  1. Stage 1 (Parallel Addition & Subtraction):
    • The plus agent subscribes to inputs A and B, publishing the sum to R1.
    • The minus agent subscribes to inputs A and B, publishing the difference to R2.
  2. Stage 2 (Multiplication):
    • The mul agent subscribes to intermediate topics R1 and R2, publishing the final product to R3.

Required Core Framework Components (Internal Dependencies):

  • Config - Lifecycle interface determining operational deployment states.
  • BinOpAgent - Mathematical strategy-driven operational agent.
  • Agent - Core framework abstraction interface.

Code Integration Example:


 MathExampleConfig config = new MathExampleConfig();
 
 // Deploy the math calculation pipeline
 config.create();
 System.out.println("Pipeline deployed: " + config.getName() + " [v" + config.getVersion() + "]");
 
 // Resolve the topic manager and publish values
 TopicManager manager = TopicManagerSingleton.get();
 manager.getTopic("A").publish(new Message(6.0));
 manager.getTopic("B").publish(new Message(2.0));
 
 // Output topic "R3" will receive: (6 + 2) * (6 - 2) = 8 * 4 = 32.0
 
 // Tear down execution resources
 config.close();
See Also:

  • BinOpAgent
  • Agent

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    MathExampleConfig()
    Constructs a MathExampleConfig instance with an empty registry of managed agents.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    close()
    Safely closes and unregisters all active agents created by this configuration, preventing resource leaks and dangling references.
    void
    create()
    Dynamically instantiates the addition, subtraction, and multiplication agents, registers them with the global TopicManager directory, and retains references for graceful disposal.
    String
    getName()
    Retrieves the unique, human-readable name of the configuration.
    int
    getVersion()
    Retrieves the structural release version number of this configuration.

    Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

    clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

  • Constructor Details

    • MathExampleConfig

      public MathExampleConfig()
      Constructs a MathExampleConfig instance with an empty registry of managed agents.

  • Method Details

    • create

      public void create()
      Dynamically instantiates the addition, subtraction, and multiplication agents, registers them with the global TopicManager directory, and retains references for graceful disposal.
      Specified by:
      create in interface Config

      Retrieves the unique, human-readable name of the configuration.

      Safely closes and unregisters all active agents created by this configuration, preventing resource leaks and dangling references.
